patterns.what_to_look_for: A user-controlled file/path/name/template/page/doc parameter flows into a filesystem read (fs.readFile/readFileSync, sendFile, PHP fopen/include, Python open(), Java new File()/Paths.get().resolve()) where the joined path is never canonicalized and asserted to stay under a fixed base directory.
source_indicators: fs.readFileSync(path.join(baseDir, req.query.file)) with no realpath containment check
res.sendFile(req.params[0]) or express.static over a user-joined path
PHP fopen/file_get_contents on $_GET[...] joined to a docroot
Python open(os.path.join(BASE, request.args['file'])) without abspath().startswith(BASE)
Java new File(baseDir, request.getParameter('name')) / Paths.get(base).resolve(userInput)
Blacklist that strips '../' once (non-recursive) instead of normalize-then-contain
blackbox_indicators: Param named file/path/name/template/page/doc/download carrying a filename
GET /download?file=../../../../etc/passwd returns root:x:0:0 content
Content-Disposition reflects the attacker-supplied filename
Differential: changing ../ depth flips response (404 vs 200 vs raw bytes)
Reading /etc/passwd, /proc/self/environ, web.config, or app source succeeds
evidence_required: Source-to-sink trace: user param -> filesystem read
Absence of canonical-path (realpath/normalize + startsWith base) containment
Live response returning out-of-root file contents (e.g. /etc/passwd)
Exact reproduction request + raw response excerpt
false_positive_checks: Confirm no normalize-then-startsWith(baseDir) guard runs after the join
Check input isn't locked to an allowlist/enum or an opaque hashed id
Rule out a WAF/framework normalizer rejecting traversal upstream
Verify returned bytes are the real target file, not a templated error echoing the path
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Request the read/download endpoint with file=../../../../etc/passwd alongside a known in-scope file and compare responses for an out-of-root read.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as path traversal/LFI naming the exact parameter, the minimal traversal payload, and the retrieved out-of-root file as proof; rate by sensitivity of files reachable from the base dir.

patterns.what_to_look_for: A user-controlled value selects an include/import/require/template target where a URL, wrapper, or absolute path can be supplied, yielding remote (RFI) or arbitrary local (LFI-to-source/RCE) inclusion via PHP include/require, dynamic require()/import(), or a template loader reading from a user path.
source_indicators: PHP include/require/include_once($_GET[...]) or fopen() on a user-supplied URL
php.ini allow_url_include=On / allow_url_fopen reachable on the include sink
Node require(userInput) or dynamic import(userVar) with attacker-controlled module path
Template engine render(userPath) loading includes from filesystem or URL (Twig/Jinja/EJS)
php://filter, data://, expect:// wrappers usable on the inclusion sink
blackbox_indicators: page/module/template/lang/view param resolving to .php/.tpl or extensionless includes
page=//attacker-host or http://attacker triggers an outbound fetch (true RFI)
page=php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=index returns base64 source
Setting the param to a known local file changes page composition
evidence_required: Source/sink trace: user input -> include/require/import
Proof the sink accepts a wrapper or remote URL (not a fixed-suffix path)
Live proof: OAST callback to attacker host OR php://filter base64 source disclosure
Reproduction request + response or out-of-band hit
false_positive_checks: Confirm value isn't suffixed/prefixed onto a fixed path that locks it to one directory
Check allow_url_include is actually On for true RFI vs LFI-only
Rule out an allowlist/switch of permitted include names
Distinguish a reflected URL echo from an actual server-side fetch
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Point the include/page param at a benign OAST URL and separately at php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=<known-file>, then watch for an outbound callback or base64 source in the response.
patterns.reporting_guidance: State RFI vs LFI explicitly, name the inclusion sink and wrapper used, and attach the OAST callback or decoded source as proof; escalate to RCE class when a remote/wrapper include executes.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Archive extraction (zip/tar/jar/docx/ipa) joins each entry name onto the output directory and writes without verifying the resolved path stays inside the extraction root, so an entry named ../../ or an absolute path escapes and overwrites arbitrary files (Zip-Slip).
source_indicators: new File(destDir, zipEntry.getName()) / Paths.get(dir, entry.name) then write, no canonical-path check
Node fs.writeFile(path.join(out, entry.fileName)) (unzipper/yauzl/adm-zip) with no normalize guard
Python zipfile.extractall()/tarfile.extractall() on untrusted archive without filter= (pre-3.12)
Missing entryPath.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(destDir) assertion before write
Archive entry name used raw as the write destination
blackbox_indicators: Feature accepts user-uploaded zip/tar/jar/docx/ipa/backup and extracts it server-side
Config/cron/webroot artifacts change after uploading a crafted archive
Import/restore/plugin-install endpoint that ingests an archive
Behavior differs between an archive with a ../ entry and a benign one
evidence_required: Extraction sink joining entry name without a containment check
Crafted archive containing a ../ (or absolute-path) entry
Proof a benign marker file was written outside the extraction root
Reproduction archive + observed out-of-root file or effect
false_positive_checks: Confirm no canonical-path / normalize-and-startsWith guard runs before write
Check the library/version doesn't reject traversal entries by default (e.g. Python 3.12 data filter)
Verify the extraction dir isn't a throwaway sandbox with no sensitive neighbors
Rule out entry-name sanitization that strips ../ first
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload an archive with one benign entry named ../<unique>.txt targeting a path you can read back, and confirm it lands outside the extraction directory.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as Zip-Slip with the crafted entry name and the out-of-root write location; escalate to RCE or config-overwrite when a sensitive sink path is writable.

patterns.what_to_look_for: File operations (archive extraction, upload move, static serving, temp/log handling) follow symlinks, so a symlink entry or a planted link redirects reads/writes outside the intended root; or a check-then-use TOCTOU gap lets the path change between validation and the operation.
source_indicators: tar/zip extractor recreates symlink entries without validating the link target (no NoFollow/realpath check)
fs operations lacking lstat/O_NOFOLLOW; realpath() result not re-validated against base dir
Static server (express.static/nginx) serving a directory where users can plant symlinks
Check-then-use gap: access()/exists() then open() on the same user-controlled path
Java Files.copy/move on an untrusted tree without LinkOption.NOFOLLOW_LINKS
blackbox_indicators: Archive import preserves symlinks; an uploaded tar with a symlink entry is then read through
After uploading a symlink-bearing archive, reading the link path returns out-of-root content
Download endpoint returns the target of a symlink planted via another feature
Race-prone endpoints (rename/move/import) intermittently read or write the wrong file
evidence_required: Sink that follows links or lacks NOFOLLOW/realpath re-check
Crafted symlink entry or a planted link
Proof of out-of-root read or write through the link (benign marker or known file)
Reproduction steps + observed effect
false_positive_checks: Confirm the extractor doesn't skip or deny symlink entries by default
Check realpath containment isn't enforced after link resolution
Verify the OS/container layer actually permits symlinks
Rule out the link target resolving inside the allowed root anyway
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Extract/upload an archive whose entry is a symlink to a benign known in-scope file, then read it back to confirm the link was followed outside the root.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report symlink-following (or TOCTOU) with the link entry/target and the resulting out-of-root read or write; note it bypasses ../-only Zip-Slip filters.

patterns.what_to_look_for: The server or framework decodes/normalizes a path so encoded or platform-specific traversal slips past a filter: double-encoding (%252e), %2e%2e%2f, overlong UTF-8 (..%c0%af), backslashes, null byte (%00), leading-slash absolute path, or an nginx 'alias' location without a trailing slash (off-by-slash).
source_indicators: Traversal filter runs on raw input but the server URL-decodes after the check (decode-order bug)
Single-pass replace('../','') or a regex that misses encoded or \..\ forms
nginx: location /static { alias /var/www/files; } with no trailing slash (alias traversal)
Path concat from request.uri without decode-then-normalize-then-contain
Null-byte or extension-append truncation in older runtimes (PHP<5.3, legacy Java File)
blackbox_indicators: ..%2f / ..%252f / %2e%2e%2f / ..%c0%af / ..\ variants return out-of-root files where plain ../ is blocked
Off-by-slash: GET /static../app.config (nginx alias) returns a parent-dir file
Leading /etc/passwd or a \\ absolute path returns file contents
%00 or ;.png appended bypasses an extension allowlist
evidence_required: Which encoding/normalization bypass actually succeeds
Decode/normalize ordering bug or alias misconfig in source/config
Live out-of-root file retrieved via the encoded payload
Reproduction request with the exact encoding + response excerpt
false_positive_checks: Confirm plain ../ is genuinely blocked so the encoded variant is the real bypass, not noise
Check the decoded body is genuine out-of-root content, not a 404/error page
Rule out a CDN/WAF normalizing before origin (test the origin directly if in scope)
Verify nginx alias vs root semantics before claiming off-by-slash
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Replay the blocked traversal using encoded variants (%2e%2e%2f, %252e, ..%c0%af, ..\, leading-slash) and the nginx off-by-slash form against a known out-of-root file.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report the specific encoding or alias bypass with the exact payload and retrieved file; flag decode-after-check ordering or an nginx alias missing its trailing slash as the root cause.

patterns.what_to_look_for: The write destination of an upload/export/save is built from a user-controlled filename or directory field, letting ../ or an absolute path redirect the write outside the intended upload directory (overwrite webroot/config/keys, chain to RCE).
source_indicators: path.join(uploadDir, req.body.filename) / file.mv(dir + req.files.name) using the raw client filename
A separate path/folder field used as the write target unchecked
Python open(os.path.join(UPLOAD, request.form['name']),'wb') with no basename()
No path.basename()/sanitize and no resolved-path containment on the write side
Content-Disposition / multipart filename trusted for the storage path
blackbox_indicators: Upload accepts a filename/path field; sending name=../../app/x.js makes the file appear served/executed elsewhere
Overwriting a known static asset via a traversal filename changes app behavior
Writing into the webroot yields a retrievable/executable file
Server honors an absolute path supplied in the filename
evidence_required: Write sink using a user-controlled filename/dir without basename + containment
Upload carrying a ../ or absolute-path filename
Proof the file landed outside the upload dir (benign marker retrieved)
Reproduction upload + the location of the written file
false_positive_checks: Confirm the server doesn't apply basename()/UUID rename before writing
Check resolved write-path containment isn't enforced
Verify the target dir is actually sensitive/served, not an isolated quota dir
Rule out a storage layer (e.g. S3 key) treating ../ as a literal key, not a path escape
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload a benign marker file with filename ../<unique>.txt (and separately an absolute path) into a directory you can read back, confirming it wrote outside the upload root.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report upload path-traversal write with the traversal filename and the out-of-root write location; escalate to RCE when the destination is an executable webroot path.
